headline: Amazon security research reportedly led to the White House’s Anthropic Fable ban
summary: Amazon's cybersecurity research reportedly triggered the White House's ban on Anthropic's AI models. CEO Andy Jassy's discussions with the White House were a contributing factor. The research claimed flaws in Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5, leading to their export control.

headline: X-Men ’97 has what Master of the Universe is missing
summary: Marvel and Mattel are both launching projects based on nostalgic animated heroes. X-Men '97's second season will explore an apocalyptic future for mutants. The article contrasts X-Men '97's approach with Masters of the Universe's live-action film.

headline: Sealed Super Mario Bros. sells for a record $3 million
summary: A sealed copy of Super Mario Bros. was auctioned for a record $3 million. This sale shatters the previous record of $2 million, also for a Super Mario Bros. copy in 2021. The auction continues a trend of high-value retro game sales.

headline: Microsoft hasn’t ruled out spinning off Xbox
summary: Microsoft is reportedly considering spinning off its Xbox division into a separate company. The company is also preparing for significant layoffs within Xbox and reevaluating Project Helix console plans. A new report from The Information details the potential dramatic restructuring.

headline: Rockstar Co-Founder Dan Houser Says There’s No Right Way to Play an Open-World Game
summary: Rockstar co-founder Dan Houser discussed open-world games at the 2026 Tribeca Festival. He asserted that players should feel free to approach open-world games without a 'right' way. Houser spoke about creating interconnected multimedia universes with Absurd Ventures co-founder Lazlow.

headline: Microsoft CEO Says 'There's More Monetization of Xbox Games Happening on YouTube Than at Microsoft'
summary: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella claims YouTube monetizes Xbox games more than Microsoft does. His comments follow Xbox boss Asha Sharma's commitment to 'reset' the gaming branch. This highlights a potential revenue gap for Xbox compared to creator content.

headline: George Miller Reportedly Wants to Make One More Mad Max Movie and a TV Show Before Selling the Franchise
summary: George Miller plans to make one final Mad Max movie and a TV show. He is reportedly seeking studios to produce these projects. Following these, Miller intends to sell the rights to the Mad Max universe.

headline: X-Men '97 Season 2, Episodes 1-4 Review (Spoiler-Free)
summary: The first four episodes of X-Men '97 Season 2 maintain the show's thrilling quality. The season continues to deliver a blend of Marvel nostalgia and deep character drama. A spoiler-free review confirms the show remains engaging.

headline: UK armed forces board Russian shadow fleet oil tanker in English Channel, PM says
summary: Royal Marine Commandos and NCA officers boarded a Russian shadow fleet oil tanker. The six-hour operation took place in the English Channel early Sunday. The Prime Minister confirmed the boarding incident.

headline: Trump says US-Iran deal to be signed on Sunday as Tehran casts doubt on timing
summary: Donald Trump announced a US-Iran deal would be signed on Sunday. Tehran, however, expressed doubt regarding the exact timing of the agreement. The discrepancy highlights uncertainty around the potential deal.

headline: Molly Russell's dad says PM rushing social media restrictions 'deplorable'
summary: Molly Russell's father criticized the Prime Minister for rushing social media restrictions. He believes the plans are being fast-tracked for 'political reasons.' His daughter took her own life after viewing harmful online content.

headline: Norway braces for verdict in rape trial of crown princess's son Høiby
summary: Norway awaits the verdict in the rape trial of Marius Borg Høiby, crown princess's son. Høiby faces 40 charges, including four counts of rape. He will appear via video link for the verdict, nearly three months after his trial concluded.
